#Feature Importance ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
importances = pd.Series(rf_final.feature_importances_,
                        index=FEATURES).sort_values(ascending=False)
plt.figure(figsize=(4, 4))
sns.barplot(x=importances.values, y=importances.index, palette='magma')
plt.title('Feature Importance — What Drives Recommendation?', fontsize=13)
plt.xlabel('Importance Score')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [27]:
# =============================================================================
# DECISION TREE VISUALIZATION FROM RANDOM FOREST
# =============================================================================
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("DECISION TREE VISUALIZATION FROM RANDOM FOREST")
print("="*60)

# ── Only these imports needed — NO DecisionTreeClassifier ─────────────────────
from sklearn.tree import plot_tree, export_text

# ── Extract single tree directly from trained Random Forest ───────────────────
# best_model is already trained in Step 11 — no new training needed
single_tree = best_model.estimators_[0]   # extract Tree #1 from RF

print(f"\n✅ Extracted Tree #1 from Random Forest")
print(f"✅ Total Trees in RF   : {len(best_model.estimators_)}")
print(f"✅ Tree Depth          : {single_tree.get_depth()}")
print(f"✅ Total Nodes         : {single_tree.tree_.node_count}")
print(f"✅ Total Leaves        : {single_tree.tree_.n_leaves}")

# ── Predict using this single tree ───────────────────────────────────────────
y_pred_tree = single_tree.predict(X_test)
acc_tree    = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred_tree)
print(f"✅ Single Tree Accuracy: {acc_tree:.4f}")
print(f"   (RF overall accuracy is higher because")
print(f"    it combines all {len(best_model.estimators_)} trees by voting)")

# ── Classification Report ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n📊 Classification Report — Single Tree from RF:")
print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred_tree,
      target_names=['No Recommend', 'Yes Recommend']))

# ── Text Rules of Tree ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n📊 DECISION TREE RULES (Text Format):")
print("="*60)
tree_rules = export_text(single_tree, feature_names=FEATURES)
tree_lines = tree_rules.split('\n')
print('\n'.join(tree_lines[:40]))
print("...(truncated for readability)...")


# =============================================================================
# CHART 1: DECISION TREE — SMALL BUT VISIBLE
# =============================================================================
plt.figure(figsize=(14, 5))
plot_tree(
    single_tree,
    feature_names = FEATURES,
    class_names   = ['No', 'Yes'],
    filled        = True,
    rounded       = True,
    fontsize      = 8,
    max_depth     = 3,
    impurity      = False          # hide gini to save space
)
plt.title(
    f'Decision Tree (Tree #1 from Random Forest) | '
    f'Acc={acc_tree:.4f} | '
    f'Nodes={single_tree.tree_.node_count} | '
    f'Leaves={single_tree.tree_.n_leaves}',
    fontsize=11, fontweight='bold'
)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
print("✅ Decision Tree chart displayed.")


# =============================================================================
# CHART 2: CONFUSION MATRIX — SINGLE TREE
# =============================================================================
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(4, 3))
cm_tree = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred_tree)
disp    = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm_tree,
                                  display_labels=['No', 'Yes'])
disp.plot(ax=ax, cmap='Blues', colorbar=False)

TN = cm_tree[0][0]; FP = cm_tree[0][1]
FN = cm_tree[1][0]; TP = cm_tree[1][1]

for i, j, label in [(0,0,f'TN={TN}'), (0,1,f'FP={FP}'),
                     (1,0,f'FN={FN}'), (1,1,f'TP={TP}')]:
    ax.text(j, i+0.35, label,
            ha='center', fontsize=8,
            color='red', fontweight='bold')

ax.set_title(f'Single Tree Confusion Matrix\nAcc={acc_tree:.4f}',
             fontsize=10)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

print(f"\n📌 TN={TN} | FP={FP} | FN={FN} | TP={TP}")
print(f"   Total Correct : {TN+TP}")
print(f"   Total Wrong   : {FP+FN}")


# ── How to Read the Tree ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("HOW TO READ THE DECISION TREE")
print("="*60)
print("""
Each box (node) shows:

┌──────────────────────────────┐
│ Rating - 10 <= 5.5           │ ← Split condition
│ samples = 1500               │ ← Samples here
│ value = [900, 600]           │ ← [No count, Yes count]
│ class = No Recommend         │ ← Majority class
└──────────────────────────────┘
        /           \\
     True           False
   (go left)      (go right)

Color:
🔵 Blue   = predicts No Recommend
🟠 Orange = predicts Yes Recommend
Darker    = more confident prediction
Lighter   = mixed/uncertain
""")

============================================================
DECISION TREE VISUALIZATION FROM RANDOM FOREST
============================================================

✅ Extracted Tree #1 from Random Forest
✅ Total Trees in RF   : 50
✅ Tree Depth          : 5
✅ Total Nodes         : 41
✅ Total Leaves        : 21
✅ Single Tree Accuracy: 0.9615
   (RF overall accuracy is higher because
    it combines all 50 trees by voting)

📊 Classification Report — Single Tree from RF:
               precision    recall  f1-score   support

 No Recommend       0.96      0.99      0.97       289
Yes Recommend       0.97      0.91      0.94       152

     accuracy                           0.96       441
    macro avg       0.96      0.95      0.96       441
 weighted avg       0.96      0.96      0.96       441


📊 DECISION TREE RULES (Text Format):
============================================================
|--- Rating - 10 <= 5.01
|   |--- AirLine_Encoded <= 3.50
|   |   |--- Review_Sentiment <= 0.61
|   |   |   |--- Rating - 10 <= 4.50
|   |   |   |   |--- Review_Sentiment <= -0.04
|   |   |   |   |   |--- class: 0.0
|   |   |   |   |--- Review_Sentiment >  -0.04
|   |   |   |   |   |--- class: 0.0
|   |   |   |--- Rating - 10 >  4.50
|   |   |   |   |--- Review_Length <= 112.00
|   |   |   |   |   |--- class: 1.0
|   |   |   |   |--- Review_Length >  112.00
|   |   |   |   |   |--- class: 0.0
|   |   |--- Review_Sentiment >  0.61
|   |   |   |--- class: 1.0
|   |--- AirLine_Encoded >  3.50
|   |   |--- Title_Sentiment <= 0.53
|   |   |   |--- Review_Sentiment <= 0.24
|   |   |   |   |--- AirLine_Encoded <= 4.50
|   |   |   |   |   |--- class: 0.0
|   |   |   |   |--- AirLine_Encoded >  4.50
|   |   |   |   |   |--- class: 0.0
|   |   |   |--- Review_Sentiment >  0.24
|   |   |   |   |--- Review_Sentiment <= 0.28
|   |   |   |   |   |--- class: 1.0
|   |   |   |   |--- Review_Sentiment >  0.28
|   |   |   |   |   |--- class: 0.0
|   |   |--- Title_Sentiment >  0.53
|   |   |   |--- Title_Sentiment <= 0.65
|   |   |   |   |--- class: 1.0
|   |   |   |--- Title_Sentiment >  0.65
|   |   |   |   |--- Review_Sentiment <= 0.22
|   |   |   |   |   |--- class: 0.0
|   |   |   |   |--- Review_Sentiment >  0.22
|   |   |   |   |   |--- class: 1.0
|--- Rating - 10 >  5.01
|   |--- Rating - 10 <= 6.01
|   |   |--- Rating - 10 <= 5.95
|   |   |   |--- class: 1.0
|   |   |--- Rating - 10 >  5.95
...(truncated for readability)...

✅ Decision Tree chart displayed.

📌 TN=285 | FP=4 | FN=13 | TP=139
   Total Correct : 424
   Total Wrong   : 17

============================================================
HOW TO READ THE DECISION TREE
============================================================

Each box (node) shows:

┌──────────────────────────────┐
│ Rating - 10 <= 5.5           │ ← Split condition
│ samples = 1500               │ ← Samples here
│ value = [900, 600]           │ ← [No count, Yes count]
│ class = No Recommend         │ ← Majority class
└──────────────────────────────┘
        /           \
     True           False
   (go left)      (go right)

Color:
🔵 Blue   = predicts No Recommend
🟠 Orange = predicts Yes Recommend
Darker    = more confident prediction
Lighter   = mixed/uncertain
In [28]:
# =============================================================================
# KNN MODEL — k=3, k=7, k=9
# =============================================================================
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("KNN MODEL — k=3, k=7, k=9")
print("="*60)

from sklearn.neighbors     import KNeighborsClassifier
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.metrics       import (accuracy_score, classification_report,
                                   confusion_matrix, ConfusionMatrixDisplay,
                                   f1_score)

# ── Scale features — KNN needs scaling ───────────────────────────────────────
scaler         = StandardScaler()
X_train_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(X_train_smote)  # SMOTE balanced
X_test_scaled  = scaler.transform(X_test)             # original test

# ── KNN k=3 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
knn3      = KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=3)
knn3.fit(X_train_scaled, y_train_smote)
y_pred_k3 = knn3.predict(X_test_scaled)
acc_k3    = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred_k3)

# ── KNN k=7 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
knn7      = KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=7)
knn7.fit(X_train_scaled, y_train_smote)
y_pred_k7 = knn7.predict(X_test_scaled)
acc_k7    = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred_k7)

# ── KNN k=9 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
knn9      = KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=9)
knn9.fit(X_train_scaled, y_train_smote)
y_pred_k9 = knn9.predict(X_test_scaled)
acc_k9    = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred_k9)

# ── Accuracy Table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n📊 KNN ACCURACY TABLE:")
print("="*45)
print(f"{'Model':<15} {'Accuracy':>10}")
print("-"*45)
print(f"{'KNN (k=3)':<15} {acc_k3:>10.4f}")
print(f"{'KNN (k=7)':<15} {acc_k7:>10.4f}")
print(f"{'KNN (k=9)':<15} {acc_k9:>10.4f}")
print("="*45)

best_k   = max({'k=3': acc_k3, 'k=7': acc_k7, 'k=9': acc_k9},
                key=lambda x: {'k=3': acc_k3,
                               'k=7': acc_k7,
                               'k=9': acc_k9}[x])
print(f"\n✅ Best KNN : {best_k} → "
      f"{max(acc_k3, acc_k7, acc_k9):.4f}")

# ── Classification Reports ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
for k, pred in [('k=3', y_pred_k3),
                ('k=7', y_pred_k7),
                ('k=9', y_pred_k9)]:
    print(f"\n📊 Classification Report — KNN {k}:")
    print(classification_report(y_test, pred,
          target_names=['No Recommend', 'Yes Recommend']))

# ── Line Chart: k=3 vs k=7 vs k=9 ───────────────────────────────────────────
plt.figure(figsize=(7, 4))
k_values = [3, 7, 9]
k_accs   = [acc_k3, acc_k7, acc_k9]

plt.plot(k_values, k_accs,
         marker='o', color='#3498db',
         linewidth=2, markersize=10)

for k, acc in zip(k_values, k_accs):
    plt.text(k, acc + 0.001,
             f'{acc:.4f}',
             ha='center', fontsize=10,
             fontweight='bold')

plt.xticks([3, 7, 9], ['k=3', 'k=7', 'k=9'], fontsize=11)
plt.xlabel('K Value', fontsize=12)
plt.ylabel('Accuracy', fontsize=12)
plt.title('KNN Accuracy: k=3 vs k=7 vs k=9', fontsize=13)
plt.ylim(min(k_accs) - 0.03, max(k_accs) + 0.03)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ── KNN Confusion Matrices (3 side by side) ───────────────────────────────────
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(14, 4))

for ax, (name, pred) in zip(axes,
        [('KNN k=3', y_pred_k3),
         ('KNN k=7', y_pred_k7),
         ('KNN k=9', y_pred_k9)]):
    cm   = confusion_matrix(y_test, pred)
    disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm,
                                   display_labels=['No', 'Yes'])
    disp.plot(ax=ax, cmap='Blues', colorbar=False)
    acc = accuracy_score(y_test, pred)

    TN = cm[0][0]; FP = cm[0][1]
    FN = cm[1][0]; TP = cm[1][1]

    for i, j, label in [(0,0,f'TN={TN}'), (0,1,f'FP={FP}'),
                         (1,0,f'FN={FN}'), (1,1,f'TP={TP}')]:
        ax.text(j, i+0.35, label,
                ha='center', fontsize=8,
                color='red', fontweight='bold')

    ax.set_title(f'{name}\nAcc={acc:.4f}',
                 fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
    ax.tick_params(labelsize=8)

plt.suptitle('KNN Confusion Matrices — k=3, k=7, k=9',
             fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
print("✅ KNN complete.")

============================================================
KNN MODEL — k=3, k=7, k=9
============================================================

📊 KNN ACCURACY TABLE:
=============================================
Model             Accuracy
---------------------------------------------
KNN (k=3)           0.9501
KNN (k=7)           0.9615
KNN (k=9)           0.9592
=============================================

✅ Best KNN : k=7 → 0.9615

📊 Classification Report — KNN k=3:
               precision    recall  f1-score   support

 No Recommend       0.95      0.98      0.96       289
Yes Recommend       0.95      0.90      0.93       152

     accuracy                           0.95       441
    macro avg       0.95      0.94      0.94       441
 weighted avg       0.95      0.95      0.95       441


📊 Classification Report — KNN k=7:
               precision    recall  f1-score   support

 No Recommend       0.96      0.99      0.97       289
Yes Recommend       0.97      0.91      0.94       152

     accuracy                           0.96       441
    macro avg       0.96      0.95      0.96       441
 weighted avg       0.96      0.96      0.96       441


📊 Classification Report — KNN k=9:
               precision    recall  f1-score   support

 No Recommend       0.96      0.98      0.97       289
Yes Recommend       0.97      0.91      0.94       152

     accuracy                           0.96       441
    macro avg       0.96      0.95      0.95       441
 weighted avg       0.96      0.96      0.96       441


✅ KNN complete.
In [29]:
# =============================================================================
# NAIVE BAYES MODEL
# =============================================================================
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("NAIVE BAYES MODEL")
print("="*60)

from sklearn.naive_bayes import GaussianNB

nb        = GaussianNB()
nb.fit(X_train_smote, y_train_smote)
y_pred_nb = nb.predict(X_test)
acc_nb    = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred_nb)

print(f"\n📊 Naive Bayes Accuracy : {acc_nb:.4f}")

print("\n📊 Classification Report — Naive Bayes:")
print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred_nb,
      target_names=['No Recommend', 'Yes Recommend']))

# ── Naive Bayes Confusion Matrix ──────────────────────────────────────────────
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(4, 3))
cm_nb   = confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred_nb)
disp    = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm_nb,
                                  display_labels=['No', 'Yes'])
disp.plot(ax=ax, cmap='Blues', colorbar=False)

TN = cm_nb[0][0]; FP = cm_nb[0][1]
FN = cm_nb[1][0]; TP = cm_nb[1][1]

for i, j, label in [(0,0,f'TN={TN}'), (0,1,f'FP={FP}'),
                     (1,0,f'FN={FN}'), (1,1,f'TP={TP}')]:
    ax.text(j, i+0.35, label,
            ha='center', fontsize=8,
            color='red', fontweight='bold')

ax.set_title(f'Naive Bayes Confusion Matrix\nAcc={acc_nb:.4f}',
             fontsize=10)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
print("✅ Naive Bayes complete.")

============================================================
NAIVE BAYES MODEL
============================================================

📊 Naive Bayes Accuracy : 0.9615

📊 Classification Report — Naive Bayes:
               precision    recall  f1-score   support

 No Recommend       0.97      0.98      0.97       289
Yes Recommend       0.95      0.93      0.94       152

     accuracy                           0.96       441
    macro avg       0.96      0.95      0.96       441
 weighted avg       0.96      0.96      0.96       441


✅ Naive Bayes complete.
In [31]:
# =============================================================================
# FINAL COMPARISON: RF vs KNN(3,7,9) vs NAIVE BAYES
# =============================================================================
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("FINAL MODEL COMPARISON")
print("="*60)

from sklearn.metrics import recall_score, precision_score

y_pred_rf = best_model.predict(X_test)
acc_rf    = accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred_rf)

all_models = {
    'Random Forest' : acc_rf,
    'KNN (k=3)'     : acc_k3,
    'KNN (k=7)'     : acc_k7,
    'KNN (k=9)'     : acc_k9,
    'Naive Bayes'   : acc_nb,
}

best_name = max(all_models, key=all_models.get)
best_acc  = max(all_models.values())

# ── Accuracy Table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n📊 FINAL ACCURACY TABLE:")
print("="*45)
print(f"{'Model':<20} {'Accuracy':>10} {'':>8}")
print("-"*45)
for name, acc in all_models.items():
    tag = '⭐ BEST' if name == best_name else ''
    print(f"{name:<20} {acc:>10.4f} {tag:>8}")
print("="*45)
print(f"\n🏆 BEST MODEL : {best_name}")
print(f"🏆 ACCURACY   : {best_acc:.4f}")

# ── Detailed Metrics Table ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n📊 DETAILED METRICS TABLE:")
print("="*75)
print(f"{'Model':<20} {'Accuracy':>10} {'F1-No':>8} "
      f"{'F1-Yes':>8} {'Rec-No':>8} {'Rec-Yes':>9}")
print("-"*75)

for name, pred in [('Random Forest', y_pred_rf),
                   ('KNN (k=3)',     y_pred_k3),
                   ('KNN (k=7)',     y_pred_k7),
                   ('KNN (k=9)',     y_pred_k9),
                   ('Naive Bayes',   y_pred_nb)]:
    acc = accuracy_score(y_test, pred)
    f1n = f1_score(y_test, pred, pos_label=0)
    f1y = f1_score(y_test, pred, pos_label=1)
    rcn = recall_score(y_test, pred, pos_label=0)
    rcy = recall_score(y_test, pred, pos_label=1)
    print(f"{name:<20} {acc:>10.4f} {f1n:>8.4f} "
          f"{f1y:>8.4f} {rcn:>8.4f} {rcy:>9.4f}")
print("="*75)

# ── Final Bar Chart ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))

names  = list(all_models.keys())
accs   = list(all_models.values())
colors = ['#e74c3c' if n == best_name else
          '#3498db' if 'KNN'   in n    else
          '#2ecc71' for n in names]

bars = plt.bar(names, accs, color=colors,
               edgecolor='black',
               linewidth=0.5, width=0.5)

for bar in bars:
    plt.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2,
             bar.get_height() + 0.001,
             f'{bar.get_height():.4f}',
             ha='center', fontsize=10,
             fontweight='bold')

best_idx = names.index(best_name)
plt.annotate(
    '⭐ BEST',
    xy=(best_idx, best_acc),
    xytext=(best_idx + 0.4, best_acc + 0.01),
    fontsize=11, fontweight='bold', color='red',
    arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='red', lw=2)
)

from matplotlib.patches import Patch
plt.legend(handles=[
    Patch(facecolor='#e74c3c', label='Best Model'),
    Patch(facecolor='#3498db', label='KNN'),
    Patch(facecolor='#2ecc71', label='Naive Bayes'),
], fontsize=10)

plt.title('Final: RF vs KNN(3,7,9) vs Naive Bayes\n'
          '(All trained on SMOTE Balanced Data)', fontsize=13)
plt.xlabel('Model', fontsize=11)
plt.ylabel('Accuracy', fontsize=11)
plt.ylim(min(accs) - 0.05, 1.0)
plt.xticks(rotation=15, fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ── All 5 Confusion Matrices Side by Side ────────────────────────────────────
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 5, figsize=(16, 4))

for ax, (name, pred) in zip(axes, [
        ('Random Forest', y_pred_rf),
        ('KNN k=3',       y_pred_k3),
        ('KNN k=7',       y_pred_k7),
        ('KNN k=9',       y_pred_k9),
        ('Naive Bayes',   y_pred_nb)]):
    cm   = confusion_matrix(y_test, pred)
    disp = ConfusionMatrixDisplay(confusion_matrix=cm,
                                   display_labels=['No', 'Yes'])
    disp.plot(ax=ax, cmap='Blues', colorbar=False)
    acc = accuracy_score(y_test, pred)
    ax.set_title(f'{name}\nAcc={acc:.4f}',
                 fontsize=9, fontweight='bold',
                 color='red' if name == best_name else 'black')
    ax.tick_params(labelsize=7)
    ax.set_xlabel('Predicted', fontsize=7)
    ax.set_ylabel('Actual',    fontsize=7)

plt.suptitle('All Models — Confusion Matrices Compared',
             fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# ── Final Conclusion ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("FINAL CONCLUSION")
print("="*60)
print(f"""
   Random Forest  : {acc_rf:.4f}
   KNN (k=3)      : {acc_k3:.4f}
   KNN (k=7)      : {acc_k7:.4f}
   KNN (k=9)      : {acc_k9:.4f}
   Naive Bayes    : {acc_nb:.4f}
{"="*60}
   🏆 BEST MODEL  : {best_name}
   🏆 ACCURACY    : {best_acc:.4f}
{"="*60}
""")

============================================================
FINAL MODEL COMPARISON
============================================================

📊 FINAL ACCURACY TABLE:
=============================================
Model                  Accuracy         
---------------------------------------------
Random Forest            0.9683   ⭐ BEST
KNN (k=3)                0.9501         
KNN (k=7)                0.9615         
KNN (k=9)                0.9592         
Naive Bayes              0.9615         
=============================================

🏆 BEST MODEL : Random Forest
🏆 ACCURACY   : 0.9683

📊 DETAILED METRICS TABLE:
===========================================================================
Model                  Accuracy    F1-No   F1-Yes   Rec-No   Rec-Yes
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
Random Forest            0.9683   0.9761   0.9527   0.9896    0.9276
KNN (k=3)                0.9501   0.9625   0.9257   0.9758    0.9013
KNN (k=7)                0.9615   0.9710   0.9424   0.9862    0.9145
KNN (k=9)                0.9592   0.9693   0.9392   0.9827    0.9145
Naive Bayes              0.9615   0.9707   0.9435   0.9758    0.9342
===========================================================================

============================================================
FINAL CONCLUSION
============================================================

   Random Forest  : 0.9683
   KNN (k=3)      : 0.9501
   KNN (k=7)      : 0.9615
   KNN (k=9)      : 0.9592
   Naive Bayes    : 0.9615
============================================================
   🏆 BEST MODEL  : Random Forest
   🏆 ACCURACY    : 0.9683
============================================================
In [32]:
# =============================================================================
# FINAL MODEL COMPARISON — RF vs KNN vs NAIVE BAYES
# ALL METRICS: ACCURACY, F1, PRECISION, RECALL
# =============================================================================
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("FINAL MODEL COMPARISON — ALL METRICS")
print("="*60)

from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, f1_score, precision_score, recall_score

# =============================================================================
# COLLECT ALL PREDICTIONS
# =============================================================================
y_pred_rf = best_model.predict(X_test)
# y_pred_k3, y_pred_k7, y_pred_k9 — from KNN cell
# y_pred_nb                        — from Naive Bayes cell

# =============================================================================
# CALCULATE ALL METRICS FOR ALL MODELS
# =============================================================================
def get_metrics(y_true, y_pred, model_name):
    return {
        'Model'        : model_name,
        'Accuracy'     : round(accuracy_score(y_true, y_pred),        4),
        'F1 - No'      : round(f1_score(y_true, y_pred, pos_label=0), 4),
        'F1 - Yes'     : round(f1_score(y_true, y_pred, pos_label=1), 4),
        'F1 - Avg'     : round(f1_score(y_true, y_pred,
                               average='weighted'),                    4),
        'Precision-No' : round(precision_score(y_true, y_pred,
                               pos_label=0),                           4),
        'Precision-Yes': round(precision_score(y_true, y_pred,
                               pos_label=1),                           4),
        'Recall-No'    : round(recall_score(y_true, y_pred,
                               pos_label=0),                           4),
        'Recall-Yes'   : round(recall_score(y_true, y_pred,
                               pos_label=1),                           4),
    }

all_metrics = [
    get_metrics(y_test, y_pred_rf, 'Random Forest'),
    get_metrics(y_test, y_pred_k3, 'KNN (k=3)'),
    get_metrics(y_test, y_pred_k7, 'KNN (k=7)'),
    get_metrics(y_test, y_pred_k9, 'KNN (k=9)'),
    get_metrics(y_test, y_pred_nb, 'Naive Bayes'),
]

metrics_df = pd.DataFrame(all_metrics)


# =============================================================================
# TABLE 1: FULL METRICS TABLE
# =============================================================================
print("\n📊 COMPLETE METRICS TABLE — ALL MODELS")
print("="*95)
print(metrics_df.to_string(index=False))
print("="*95)


# =============================================================================
# TABLE 2: BEST MODEL PER METRIC
# =============================================================================
print("\n📊 BEST MODEL PER METRIC:")
print("="*55)
print(f"{'Metric':<20} {'Best Model':>20} {'Score':>10}")
print("-"*55)

metric_cols = ['Accuracy', 'F1 - Avg',
               'F1 - No', 'F1 - Yes',
               'Precision-No', 'Precision-Yes',
               'Recall-No',    'Recall-Yes']

for col in metric_cols:
    best_idx   = metrics_df[col].idxmax()
    best_model_name = metrics_df.loc[best_idx, 'Model']
    best_score = metrics_df[col].max()
    print(f"{col:<20} {best_model_name:>20} {best_score:>10.4f}")
print("="*55)


# =============================================================================
# CHART 1: ACCURACY COMPARISON — ALL MODELS
# =============================================================================
model_names = metrics_df['Model'].tolist()
accuracies  = metrics_df['Accuracy'].tolist()
best_acc_model = metrics_df.loc[metrics_df['Accuracy'].idxmax(), 'Model']

colors = ['#e74c3c' if m == best_acc_model else
          '#3498db' if 'KNN'  in m else
          '#2ecc71' if 'Naive' in m else
          '#9b59b6'
          for m in model_names]

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 5))
bars = plt.bar(model_names, accuracies,
               color=colors, edgecolor='black',
               linewidth=0.5, width=0.5)

for bar in bars:
    plt.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2,
             bar.get_height() + 0.001,
             f'{bar.get_height():.4f}',
             ha='center', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')

best_idx = model_names.index(best_acc_model)
plt.annotate('⭐ BEST',
    xy=(best_idx, metrics_df['Accuracy'].max()),
    xytext=(best_idx + 0.4,
            metrics_df['Accuracy'].max() + 0.01),
    fontsize=11, fontweight='bold', color='red',
    arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='red', lw=2))

from matplotlib.patches import Patch
plt.legend(handles=[
    Patch(facecolor='#e74c3c', label='Best Model'),
    Patch(facecolor='#9b59b6', label='Random Forest'),
    Patch(facecolor='#3498db', label='KNN'),
    Patch(facecolor='#2ecc71', label='Naive Bayes'),
], fontsize=9)

plt.title('Accuracy Comparison — All Models', fontsize=13,
          fontweight='bold')
plt.ylabel('Accuracy', fontsize=12)
plt.xlabel('Model',    fontsize=12)
plt.ylim(min(accuracies) - 0.05, 1.0)
plt.xticks(rotation=15, fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()


# =============================================================================
# CHART 2: F1 SCORE COMPARISON — No vs Yes FOR EACH MODEL
# =============================================================================
x     = np.arange(len(model_names))
width = 0.35

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5))
bars1 = ax.bar(x - width/2, metrics_df['F1 - No'],
               width, label='F1 - No Recommend',
               color='#e74c3c', edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)
bars2 = ax.bar(x + width/2, metrics_df['F1 - Yes'],
               width, label='F1 - Yes Recommend',
               color='#2ecc71', edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)

for bar in bars1:
    ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2,
            bar.get_height() + 0.001,
            f'{bar.get_height():.4f}',
            ha='center', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold')
for bar in bars2:
    ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2,
            bar.get_height() + 0.001,
            f'{bar.get_height():.4f}',
            ha='center', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold')

ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels(model_names, rotation=15, fontsize=9)
ax.set_ylabel('F1 Score', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('F1 Score Comparison — No vs Yes Recommend\nFor All Models',
             fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(fontsize=10)
ax.set_ylim(min(metrics_df['F1 - No'].min(),
                metrics_df['F1 - Yes'].min()) - 0.05, 1.0)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()


# =============================================================================
# CHART 3: PRECISION COMPARISON — No vs Yes
# =============================================================================
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5))
bars1 = ax.bar(x - width/2, metrics_df['Precision-No'],
               width, label='Precision - No',
               color='#3498db', edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)
bars2 = ax.bar(x + width/2, metrics_df['Precision-Yes'],
               width, label='Precision - Yes',
               color='#e67e22', edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)

for bar in bars1:
    ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2,
            bar.get_height() + 0.001,
            f'{bar.get_height():.4f}',
            ha='center', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold')
for bar in bars2:
    ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2,
            bar.get_height() + 0.001,
            f'{bar.get_height():.4f}',
            ha='center', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold')

ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels(model_names, rotation=15, fontsize=9)
ax.set_ylabel('Precision Score', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('Precision Comparison — No vs Yes Recommend\nFor All Models',
             fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(fontsize=10)
ax.set_ylim(min(metrics_df['Precision-No'].min(),
                metrics_df['Precision-Yes'].min()) - 0.05, 1.0)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()


# =============================================================================
# CHART 4: RECALL COMPARISON — No vs Yes
# =============================================================================
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5))
bars1 = ax.bar(x - width/2, metrics_df['Recall-No'],
               width, label='Recall - No',
               color='#9b59b6', edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)
bars2 = ax.bar(x + width/2, metrics_df['Recall-Yes'],
               width, label='Recall - Yes',
               color='#1abc9c', edgecolor='black', linewidth=0.5)

for bar in bars1:
    ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2,
            bar.get_height() + 0.001,
            f'{bar.get_height():.4f}',
            ha='center', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold')
for bar in bars2:
    ax.text(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width()/2,
            bar.get_height() + 0.001,
            f'{bar.get_height():.4f}',
            ha='center', fontsize=8, fontweight='bold')

ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels(model_names, rotation=15, fontsize=9)
ax.set_ylabel('Recall Score', fontsize=12)
ax.set_title('Recall Comparison — No vs Yes Recommend\nFor All Models',
             fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
ax.legend(fontsize=10)
ax.set_ylim(min(metrics_df['Recall-No'].min(),
                metrics_df['Recall-Yes'].min()) - 0.05, 1.0)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()


# =============================================================================
# CHART 5: HEATMAP — ALL METRICS ALL MODELS
# =============================================================================
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 5))

heatmap_df = metrics_df.set_index('Model')[
    ['Accuracy', 'F1 - Avg', 'F1 - No', 'F1 - Yes',
     'Precision-No', 'Precision-Yes',
     'Recall-No',    'Recall-Yes']
]

sns.heatmap(
    heatmap_df,
    annot      = True,
    fmt        = '.4f',
    cmap       = 'RdYlGn',
    linewidths = 0.5,
    vmin       = 0.7,
    vmax       = 1.0,
    annot_kws  = {'size': 9}
)
plt.title('All Metrics Heatmap — RF vs KNN vs Naive Bayes\n'
          '(Green = Better, Red = Worse)',
          fontsize=13, fontweight='bold')
plt.xticks(rotation=30, fontsize=9)
plt.yticks(rotation=0,  fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
print("✅ Heatmap displayed.")


# =============================================================================
# FINAL WINNER SUMMARY
# =============================================================================
print("\n" + "="*60)
print("🏆 FINAL WINNER — BEST MODEL PER METRIC")
print("="*60)

winners = {}
for col in ['Accuracy', 'F1 - Avg', 'F1 - No', 'F1 - Yes',
            'Precision-No', 'Precision-Yes',
            'Recall-No', 'Recall-Yes']:
    best_idx        = metrics_df[col].idxmax()
    winners[col]    = metrics_df.loc[best_idx, 'Model']

print(f"\n   Accuracy      → 🏆 {winners['Accuracy']}")
print(f"   F1 Score(avg) → 🏆 {winners['F1 - Avg']}")
print(f"   F1 - No       → 🏆 {winners['F1 - No']}")
print(f"   F1 - Yes      → 🏆 {winners['F1 - Yes']}")
print(f"   Precision-No  → 🏆 {winners['Precision-No']}")
print(f"   Precision-Yes → 🏆 {winners['Precision-Yes']}")
print(f"   Recall-No     → 🏆 {winners['Recall-No']}")
print(f"   Recall-Yes    → 🏆 {winners['Recall-Yes']}")

# Overall winner — model that wins most metrics
from collections import Counter
win_counts = Counter(winners.values())
overall_winner = win_counts.most_common(1)[0][0]
overall_wins   = win_counts.most_common(1)[0][1]

print(f"\n{'='*60}")
print(f"🥇 OVERALL BEST MODEL : {overall_winner}")
print(f"   Won {overall_wins} out of 8 metrics")
print(f"{'='*60}")

# Print win count for all models
print("\n📊 Win Count Per Model:")
print("-"*35)
for model, count in win_counts.most_common():
    bar = '█' * count
    print(f"   {model:<20} {bar} ({count})")
print("-"*35)

============================================================
FINAL MODEL COMPARISON — ALL METRICS
============================================================

📊 COMPLETE METRICS TABLE — ALL MODELS
===============================================================================================
        Model  Accuracy  F1 - No  F1 - Yes  F1 - Avg  Precision-No  Precision-Yes  Recall-No  Recall-Yes
Random Forest    0.9683   0.9761    0.9527    0.9680        0.9630         0.9792     0.9896      0.9276
    KNN (k=3)    0.9501   0.9625    0.9257    0.9498        0.9495         0.9514     0.9758      0.9013
    KNN (k=7)    0.9615   0.9710    0.9424    0.9612        0.9564         0.9720     0.9862      0.9145
    KNN (k=9)    0.9592   0.9693    0.9392    0.9589        0.9562         0.9653     0.9827      0.9145
  Naive Bayes    0.9615   0.9707    0.9435    0.9614        0.9658         0.9530     0.9758      0.9342
===============================================================================================

=======================================================
Metric                         Best Model      Score
-------------------------------------------------------
Accuracy                    Random Forest     0.9683
F1 - Avg                    Random Forest     0.9680
F1 - No                     Random Forest     0.9761
F1 - Yes                    Random Forest     0.9527
Precision-No                  Naive Bayes     0.9658
Precision-Yes               Random Forest     0.9792
Recall-No                   Random Forest     0.9896
Recall-Yes                    Naive Bayes     0.9342
=======================================================

✅ Heatmap displayed.

============================================================
🏆 FINAL WINNER — BEST MODEL PER METRIC
============================================================

   Accuracy      → 🏆 Random Forest
   F1 Score(avg) → 🏆 Random Forest
   F1 - No       → 🏆 Random Forest
   F1 - Yes      → 🏆 Random Forest
   Precision-No  → 🏆 Naive Bayes
   Precision-Yes → 🏆 Random Forest
   Recall-No     → 🏆 Random Forest
   Recall-Yes    → 🏆 Naive Bayes

   Won 6 out of 8 metrics
